title: Training (Teacher Forcing, Loss, Warmup)
summary: Encoder-decoder trains end-to-end with teacher forcing. Cross-entropy loss. AdamW with warmup. Warmup prevents early collapse when attention is random.
key_insights: Teacher forcing: decoder input = target shifted right, all positions in ONE forward pass
Loss = average cross-entropy across target positions
Gradients flow backward through entire architecture: decoder → cross-attention → encoder
Exposure bias: training sees correct tokens, inference sees own predictions
Warmup critical: random attention + SGD noise = destabilizing at initialization
Original: linear warmup + 1/√t decay. Modern: linear warmup + cosine decay

mathematical_core.loss: L = -(1/T) Σ_{t=1..T} log P(y_t | y_{<t}, X_src)

derivations.title: AdamW decomposition — what each component does

steps.name: Momentum (m_t)
steps.math: m_t = β₁·m_{t-1} + (1-β₁)·g_t. Exponential moving average of gradients. Smooths noisy SGD updates. β₁=0.9 means ~10-step memory.

steps.name: Adaptive learning rate (v_t)
steps.math: v_t = β₂·v_{t-1} + (1-β₂)·g_t². EMA of squared gradients. Parameters with consistently large gradients get smaller effective LR. β₂=0.999 means ~1000-step memory.

steps.name: Bias correction
steps.math: m̂_t = m_t/(1-β₁ᵗ), v̂_t = v_t/(1-β₂ᵗ). At t=1: m₁ = 0.1·g₁ without correction, but should be ≈g₁. Correction factor (1-β₁ᵗ) → 0.1 at t=1, compensating.

steps.name: Decoupled weight decay
steps.math: AdamW: θ -= η·λ·θ (weight decay applied directly to params, NOT through gradient). Adam (original): λ·θ added to gradient before adaptive scaling — coupling with v_t distorts decay rate.

derivations.consequence: AdamW = momentum (smooth direction) + adaptive LR (per-parameter scale) + bias correction (early stability) + decoupled decay (proper regularization). This combination is why it works for transformers.

derivations.title: Why warmup is necessary — interaction with Adam's moment estimates

steps.name: At initialization (t=1)
steps.math: m₁ = 0.1·g₁ (after bias correction ≈ g₁). v₁ = 0.001·g₁² (after correction ≈ g₁²). Effective update ≈ η · g₁/|g₁| = η · sign(g₁). Direction OK but magnitude = η regardless of gradient scale.

steps.name: Problem with attention
steps.math: At init, attention weights are random (uniform-ish). Gradients are essentially noise. Large η × noisy direction = large random step. Can push model into bad region permanently.

steps.name: Warmup solution
steps.math: Start with η ≈ 0, linearly increase to η_max over warmup_steps. During warmup: v_t accumulates gradient statistics → adaptive LR becomes reliable. Attention patterns begin to form → gradients become meaningful.

derivations.consequence: Warmup gives Adam time to calibrate its per-parameter learning rates (v_t) before taking large steps. Without it, early updates are blind large steps with poorly calibrated magnitudes.

derivations.title: Teacher forcing — why parallel training works
derivations.math: Target: [<s>, y₁, y₂, ..., y_T]. Decoder input: [<s>, y₁, ..., y_{T-1}]. Expected output: [y₁, y₂, ..., y_T]. Causal mask ensures position t sees only positions 1..t. Each position independently predicts next token. All T predictions computed in ONE pass. Loss averaged over all T.
derivations.consequence: T× speedup over sequential generation. But creates exposure bias: model never sees its own (potentially wrong) predictions during training.

monitoring: Per-position loss: first token hardest, should decrease with position
Gradient norms: encoder vs decoder — encoder grads vanishing = cross-attention not propagating back
Attention entropy over training: uniform (random init) → peaked (learned)
Validation vs training loss: divergence = overfitting
geometric_properties: Loss landscape has many minima — warmup helps find flat ones (connects to SGD noise topic)
Monitor gradient direction consistency: cosine similarity between consecutive gradient steps
statistical_properties: Adam's v_t estimates per-parameter gradient variance — monitor for stability
Learning rate effective value = η / (√v̂_t + ε) — varies per parameter
numerical_properties: AdamW stores m and v per parameter — 3× model memory
For 100M params: model=400MB, optimizer=800MB, total=1.2GB in float32
Mixed precision: model float16 (200MB), optimizer float32 (800MB)
